pages.title: What if your next career move is closer than you think? | Myskillsfuture.gov.sg
pages.text:
Education, Career and Personal Development

	What if your next career move is closer than you think?
	Published by: SkillsFuture Singapore

After years of working, there may be moments when we stop and ask:
Is it time for a career change?
It might be a feeling of restlessness. Or watching our industry change around us. Or simply sensing that there is still more that we want to do.
But change can be a scary word as we move into mid-career stage.
· What will the money be like?
· Can I afford to start over?
· How can I compete with the others out there?
Stop the guesswork, here are steps we can take to move forward.

1. Have an honest conversation with yourself
Before we start looking at job listings or courses, think about what we actually want.

Which parts of our current work do we enjoy?
Which parts drain us?
Is it the nature of the work itself, the environment, or simply the feeling that we have outgrown where we are?

Tough questions to figure out on our own?
Try Career Kaki, an AI-powered career guidance tool built by GovTech. By logging in with your Singpass, Career Kaki draws on our career and education history to provide personalised career advice and recommendations. We can also upload our resumes to get an assessment of where we are at.
Log in with Singpass at Career Kaki - personalised AI-powered career coaching.

 2. Get real market data, starting with the money
Once we have decided to look into a particular role, the first practical question is usually about finances and medium-term demand.

What will the pay be like for this new role?
Am I moving into a role where salaries have shown upward or downward trends?
Is there growing or shrinking demand for the role?

Start with the Careers & Skills Passport (CSP) Insights.
It gives us median salary, occupation demand, and job postings for our current role, or other roles we might be interested in. This sets realistic expectations about potential career moves.
The good news is that CSP Insights has just been updated with 2025 data (where available), giving us the latest view of occupation and skills trends.

3. See your skills and experience differently
Many of us underestimate the value of the critical core skills we have built over the years. For example, communication, problem solving, and learning agility are highly applicable skills across industries.
When we combine these skills with targeted training or certifications, they can open doors to new opportunities.

 On CSP, skills are presented visually so that you can quickly see your strengths and any gaps. This will help us decide what to work on next.

4. Make your verified records visible on leading job portals
CSP brings together your Government-verified qualifications, training history, and work experience.
When you apply for jobs through participating job portals, share verified records directly with recruiters and employers, which can improve shortlisting chances.
Participating online job portals:

5. Tap on support for Singaporeans
There are many programmes and initiatives available to support Singaporeans.
To understand more about the various options, speak to a Skills Ambassador.

Take the first step today
Careers often contain ups and downs, but a mid-career move is never about starting from zero. It is about understanding and repositioning what we already have.
Whatever stage we are at, the tools and support are here.
Let’s take it one step at a time by logging in with your Singpass at myskillsfuture.gov.sg/csp.

pages.text:
Education, Career and Personal Development

	Skills Demand for the Future Economy Report 2022

SkillsFuture Singapore published the inaugural Skills Demand for the Future Economy Report in 2021 with the aim of encouraging citizens:

to be aware of global trends and in-demand skills that impact their career growth, and
to take concrete actions to acquire the right skills.

The second edition of the Skills Demand for the Future Economy Report was launched in November 2022 to provide updated enriched quantitative analyses of priority skills for the following three key growth areas:

The Digital Economy
The Green Economy
The Care Economy

In addition, the Skills Demand for the Future Economy 2022 report also examines:

the jobs and skills associated with Industry 4.0 Implementation,
upskilling and reskilling needs for mid-career workers, and
an enhanced study on Critical Core Skills that individuals may need, based on the nature of their work.

You can use key insights in the Skills Demand for the Future Economy 2022 report to plan your skills development journey. Click here for more information.

pages.title: SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme | Myskillsfuture.gov.sg
pages.text:
Education, Career and Personal Development

	SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme
	Published by SkillsFuture Singapore

 What is it?
The SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme, through enhanced course fee support and training allowance, helps mid-career Singapore Citizens aged 40 years and above pursue a substantive skills reboot and adapt to a changing economy.

 How will I benefit?
If you are a Singapore Citizen aged 40 years and above, you are eligible for:

subsidies for course fees, and
training allowance.

1. Increased Course Fee Subsidy
There are two kinds of financial subsidies for course fees:

a. Mid-Career Enhanced Subsidy (MCES) for publicly-funded full-time Diploma
The MCES covers up to 90% of course fees for courses funded by the Ministry of Education (MOE) and SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG).
From Academic Year of 2025, if you enrol in a full-time Diploma from ITE (Technical/Technical Engineering Diploma), Polytechnics, and Arts Institutions (NAFA, LASALLE), you will be eligible for the MCES, on top of the prevailing base subsidies.
The list of full-time diplomas and application periods for admission can be found on the institutions’ websites.
For more information, see SkillsFuture Funding for Individuals.

b. SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career)

SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career) of $4,000, which does not expire, can further offset nett course fees for selected courses that have better employability outcomes. This is provided to all Singapore Citizens upon reaching 40 years old.
For more information, see SkillsFuture Credit.

 2. Financial Support for Long-Form Training

 Eligible individuals can tap on a maximum of 24 months of the SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance, across both full-time and part-time training.

SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance
You can apply for full-time Training Allowance if you enrol in an eligible full-time, long-form programme. This will help to cushion some of the impact from loss of income when you take time off work to train full-time.
To qualify, you will need to have earned income in the last 12 months. The monthly training allowance is 50% of one’s average income (based on government sources) over the latest 12 months leading up to course start date, with a minimum allowance of $300 and maximum allowance of $3,000.

[New!] Part-time Training Allowance
From 1 March 2026, the SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance will be extended to selected part-time training, with applications opening from 9 February 2026. To qualify, you will need to have earned income in the last 12 months. Eligible individuals who opt to continue working while pursuing part-time training can receive a flat rate of $300 per month to defray incidental training expenses.
For more information, see SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance.

 What are the eligible courses?

Explore the eligible courses for each type of support.

Type of Support

Eligible Courses

Mid-Career Enhanced Subsidy
for publicly-funded full-time Diploma

Publicly-funded full-time Diploma at Polytechnics, Institute of Technical Education, and Arts Institutions (Nanyang Academy of Fine Arts and LASALLE College of the Arts)

SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career)

Institutes of Higher Learning (IHL) full qualifications and shorter modules that stack to a full qualification
MOE-subsidised full qualifications from the University of the Arts Singapore and Arts Institutions
SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme (SCTP), which are train-and-place programmes in sectors with good hiring opportunities
Courses aligned to training requirements in Progressive Wage Model (PWM) sectors
Workplace Literacy and Numeracy courses

View full list of eligible courses here.

SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance

Eligible courses are curated high-quality programmes that facilitate good employment outcomes.
Courses eligible for Full-Time Training Allowance:

Full-time SCTP;
Full-time IHL full qualifications up to undergraduate degree-level; and
Full-time MOE-subsidised full qualifications offered by University of the Arts Singapore and Arts Institutions (up to undergraduate degree-level).

View courses eligible for Full-Time Training Allowance here
Courses eligible for Part-Time Training Allowance:

Part-time SCTP
Part-time Full Qualifications up to the undergraduate degree-level offered by the Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs), and micro-credentials that can be stacked to these Full Qualifications

View courses eligible for Part-Time Training Allowance here

 How should I proceed?
Whether you are deepening skills in your current career or reskilling for new opportunities, you can make use of these resources to develop your action plan.

Skills and Career Advisory Services

These advisory services help you identify your training needs and explore options to strengthen your career health.

Careers & Skill Passport

Explore your Careers & Skills Passport, which provides a consolidated view of your available careers and skills data from Government verified sources. Take stock of your skills and work experiences to understand what you need next to support your professional development and growth.

Career Kaki

This free AI-powered training and career digital advisor offers personalised advice with recommendations for job listings, upskilling courses, and other useful resources. Career Kaki is backed by verified government data and available 24/7.

Frequently Asked Questions

Visit Frequently Asked Questions about the SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme here.

pages.title: SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme | Myskillsfuture.gov.sg
pages.text:
Education, Career and Personal Development

	SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme
	Published by: SkillsFuture Singapore

What is SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme?

The SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme (SCTP) supports mid-career individuals in acquiring industry-relevant skills to improve employability and pivot to new sectors or job roles.
This train-and-place programme is available on a part-time or full-time format, typically ranging from three to 12 months.
The SCTP will be delivered by Continuing Education and Training (CET) Centres and is supported with employment facilitation services.
Courses under the SCTP have been progressively launched since Apr 2022. We target to have at least 200 SCTP courses by 2025.

 Key Benefits

Enroll for industry-relevant courses in sectors with good hiring opportunities such as Infocomm and Technology, Professional Services and Advanced Manufacturing.

Flexibility of part-time or full-time courses

Modular training courses of typically three to 12 months duration

Employment facilitation services and career advisory to enhance your employment prospects.

Top FAQs

 Who is eligible for SCTP? Are both unemployed jobseekers and individuals who are in-employment but seeking career transitions eligible for SCTP?

 The SCTP is open to all Singaporeans and Singapore Permanent Residents aged 21 years old and above, who are looking for training opportunities to transit to a new sector or job role. Both unemployed and employed individuals can participate in SCTP courses, as long as they can fulfil training commitments, which include the passing of assessments.

 Will trainees need to have certain minimum academic qualifications to sign up for the SCTP?

 Trainees will be assessed by the training providers on their suitability for the selected courses prior to enrolment and academic qualifications may be one of the assessment criteria.

 Will the SCTP lead to job placements?

 As SCTP operates on a Train-and-Place modality, training providers will provide career advisory and employment assistance to help trainees in their job search in relevant sectors or job roles. However, there is no guarantee of a job placement and trainees would need to be open to the job opportunities available.

 Do trainees have to pay for the SCTP? If yes, how much are the course fees and funding available?

 Yes. The exact course fees vary depending on the type of course. Nevertheless, SSG will work with training providers to ensure that course fees remain affordable.
The prevailing course fee funding rates for courses offered by SSG-appointed CET Centres will apply, i.e. eligible trainees will receive baseline funding of up to 70% of course fees, while eligible Singaporean trainees aged 40 years and above will receive enhanced funding of up to 90% of course fees under the SkillsFuture Mid-career Enhanced Subsidy.
In addition, individuals who are eligible for the additional course fee funding support will receive up to 95% course fee funding for the SCTP courses. Trainees can also use the SkillsFuture Credit and Additional SkillsFuture Credit (Mid-Career Support) to offset the out-of-pocket course fees.

 Who is eligible for the additional course fee funding support under SCTP?

 The additional course fee funding support of up to 95% of the course fees for SCTP courses will be available for jobseekers with greater needs. To be eligible for the additional course fee funding support, individuals must be Singapore Citizens and meet one of the following eligibility criteria:
a. Long-term unemployed individuals (unemployed for six months or more); or
b. Individuals in need of financial assistance – ComCare Short-to-Medium Term Assistance (SMTA) recipients or Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) recipients; or
c. Persons with Disabilities.

pages.title: SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance | Myskillsfuture.gov.sg
pages.text:
Education, Career and Personal Development

	SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance
	Published by: SkillsFuture Singapore

What is it?
SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance supports eligible mid-career Singaporeans to upskill and reskill by providing training allowance for selected long-form training.
Who is it for?
All Singapore Citizens aged 40 and above

 At a Glance
The SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance supports both full-time and part-time long-form training, and you can decide which type of training best suits your needs.
The amount of training allowance and respective implementation dates for full-time and part-time training programmes vary.

Full-time Training Allowance

Part-time Training Allowance

Who is this for?

Individuals who take time off work to pursue full-time, long-form training (usually during working hours)
Note: Only individuals with income in the latest 12 months before the course start date will be eligible for training allowance.

Employed individuals who take up part-time, long-form training (usually outside working hours)

Note: Only individuals with income in the latest 12 months before the course start date or the date of training allowance application, whichever is later, will be eligible for training allowance.

When will the scheme start?

Applications are open.

Applications are now open.

How many months of training allowance is an individual eligible for?

Eligible individuals can tap on a maximum of 24 months of training allowance over their lifetime.

How much training allowance is an individual eligible for?

50% of your average earned monthly income in the latest 12 months* before course start date, with a minimum allowance of $300 and maximum allowance of $3,000 per month
*based on government records

Flat rate of $300 per month

What are the courses eligible for training allowance?

The full-time long-form training courses are:
1. Full-time SkillsFuture Career Transition Programmes (SCTP)
2. Full-time Full Qualifications up to the undergraduate degree-level offered by the Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs)
3. Full-time MOE-subsidised Full Qualifications up to the undergraduate degree-level offered by the University of the Arts Singapore and Arts Institutions

The part-time long-form training courses are:
1. Part-time SCTP
2. Part-time Full Qualifications up to the undergraduate degree-level offered by the Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs), and micro-credentials that can be stacked to these Full Qualifications

What other requirements are there?

Meet monthly attendance or training requirements, depending on the course

How to Start
1. Identify an eligible course on MySkillsFuture.
Search and filter eligible courses on MySkillsFuture. Here’s a guide on how to do so.
Tip: Go to “Featured Initiatives” on the left and select “SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance (Full-Time)” and/or “SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance (Part-Time)” box(es) to find the eligible courses:
You can also view the full list of eligible courses here.

Still unsure? Talk to our Skills Ambassador for skills and career advice or go to the Insights page on Careers & Skills Passport to find out what’s in-demand, before you commit to your long-form training programme.
2. Submit your application.

Follow the application journey below or watch this video to make sure your application can be processed smoothly.

 Submit your application through this link via Singpass login. Use this checklist to complete your application.
Useful links

View Frequently Asked Questions about SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance here.
Wondering if you’re eligible for the Mid-Career Training Allowance and the steps to apply? Chat with SSG-WSG's WhatsApp — and help us improve by sharing your feedback!

pages.title: Will AI Take Over Your Job? How to Stay Relevant in the AI Age | Myskillsfuture.gov.sg
pages.text:
Education, Career and Personal Development

	Will AI Take Over Your Job? How to Stay Relevant in the AI Age
	Published by SkillsFuture Singapore

We all know that Artificial Intelligence (AI) is here to stay, but what does it mean for us and our jobs?

Will AI Replace Our Jobs?
It doesn’t matter if you are in a HR, Marketing, or Sales job as AI can be applied to different job functions and aspects of a business.
According to a recent global survey by McKinsey, more than two-thirds of businesses worldwide are using AI and applying it in more areas than before.

In another 2024 survey by Duke University, it found that nearly 40% of the companies have already automated tasks that employees used to do and more companies plan to do so in the next 12 months. Locally, a poll by SGTech found that 76% of respondents in the tech sector intend to build GenAI capabilities in 2024.

Will We Run Out Of Work To Do?
Many of us might feel concerned after seeing the survey results. AI can indeed replace some tasks we do. However, Agoda’s Chief Technology Officer Idan Zalzberg made a good point when he said that we should not be limited by the idea of “fixed work”.
In a CNA podcast, Zalzberg shared, “What we do today is not everything that we want to do. [It] is a fraction, a tiny fraction, of what we want to do. We want to do much more we just can't afford because we need to pay so many people so much money, we can't afford, as a business to do it. So if now we have our people being able to be two times, four times, 10 times more efficient, we actually can afford to do more.”

 We Can Be Better And Faster
While AI is a disruptive technology that will replace some jobs, it will also transform existing jobs or create new ones. Just as calculators help us calculate faster and more accurately, AI has the potential to help us achieve more as individuals.
For example, nurses can now save up to 10 minutes per patient with an AI system that can predict when a patient might leave the bed. These 10 minutes can then be used to attend to other patients. This is invaluable in the healthcare industry where every minute is precious and can be used to save more lives.

 AI is changing fast, and staying up to date is important. In Singapore, at least two in five workers use generative AI in their work according to an online survey by Salesforce and YouGov.
Here are two steps you can take to stay relevant in the age of AI.

1. Start with a Problem at Work
Find a specific problem or inefficiency in your work. Are there any ways that AI can be used to solve or improve the issue? You can start with a small pilot project to test possible solutions. For example, if you’re in marketing, consider using AI tools such as ChatGPT and Midjourney for content creation.
By first targeting a pain point and then exploring AI solutions, you might achieve surprisingly positive results.
2. Upskill with Relevant AI Courses
According to a generative AI study by Salesforce and YouGov, most business leaders (98%) agree that skills-based hiring is beneficial for their companies. However, only 1 in 10 are trained in the highly sought-after AI skills. Specifically, in Singapore, there was a 97% increase in employer demand for AI application skill from 2021 to 2022!
For many of us, learning how to become a smarter user of AI is a good place to start. We have curated some AI courses that are eligible for SkillsFuture Credit for four AI Learner Profiles below.

 What Is Your AI Learner Profile?

 Absolute Beginner
You have no prior knowledge of AI and would like to start with the basics.

 Rising Star
You have some knowledge of AI and want to learn how you can contribute more in your current role.

 Proficient Explorer
You understand AI well and want to refine your skills. These courses could help you get ahead.

 Aspiring AI Professional
You want to pursue a career in AI and need specialised courses that will equip you with the skills to get started in this field.

 Absolute Beginner

 AI and Machine Learning Begins With Me
Learn what AI is, its uses, and basic terms like Machine Learning and Deep Learning. You will work on simple projects to see how AI works in business and daily life.

 Artificial Intelligence Basics for Beginners
This course covers the basics of machine learning, including types of learning methods, model evaluation, neural networks, deep learning tools, and real-world applications.

 Artificial Intelligence for Everyone - A Practical Experience
This workshop with a hands-on approach helps you understand what AI is and shows you how to use AI in your daily life and work.

 Be a Smart Digital Citizen
This is a very basic course that explains new technologies such as AI, cloud computing, mobile technology, and various applications such as Zoom and HealthHub SG.

 Introduction to Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
Learn the basics of AI, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning. It includes practical sessions that will help you understand how they apply to businesses.

 Back to Profile Selection

 Rising Star

 Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Boosting Productivity
Learn how to use various AI tools to improve efficiency, automate tasks, boost productivity, integrate AI into workflows, and avoid common pitfalls.

 Drive Productivity with Technology (AI ChatGPT)
Learn how to use ChatGPT to drive productivity at work.

 Generative AI Prompt Engineering for Skills & Productivity Enhancement
Learn how to design prompts and context injection, evaluate and select AI models, and apply AI solutions effectively in your workplace.

 AI for Business Communication
In this short course, you will learn practical ways to use AI for improving business communication, from everyday emailing to creating personalised digital marketing content.

 Making Generative AI and ChatGPT Work for You
Explore how to use AI to create text, code, images, video, and audio, and how to use these skills to benefit your organisation.

 Apply Generative Artificial Intelligence for Your Industry
This course teaches you to use generative AI tools to solve industry-specific problems, automate tasks, improve product design, and generate new ideas.

 Back to Profile Selection

 Proficient Explorer

 AI and Machine Learning Begins With Me
Learn what AI is, its uses, and basic terms like Machine Learning and Deep Learning. You will work on simple projects to see how AI works in business and daily life.

 Mastering Intelligent Automation (IA) To Boost Your Business
In this three-day programme, you'll learn about Intelligent Automation (IA), its key technologies, and how to manage IA transformations to gain a competitive advantage and deliver business value.

 AI for Leaders: Leveraging Data Analytics for Business
Learn how to use big data analytics, machine learning, and AI to innovate and keep your business competitive.

 Develop Large Language Model (LLM) Applications with OpenAI API and Langchain
This course is about developing applications with Large Language Models (LLMs) using the OpenAI API and LangChain. You'll gain hands-on experience and learn how to create innovative AI solutions that enhance natural language processing and improve operational efficiency in your organisation.

 Certified AI Business Professional (AI Ethics & Governance)
Become proficient in AI business practices. This course focuses on generative AI and its applications (e.g. ChatGPT and Microsoft Co-pilot). You will also learn how to responsibly and ethically implement AI technologies in your workplace, handle ethical dilemmas, and comply with relevant regulations.

 Back to Profile Selection

 Aspiring AI Professional

 AWS Certified Machine Learning
This course prepares you for AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty Certification Exam. It covers the skills and practical experience needed to apply machine learning services to resolve business problems.

 Associate AI/ML Developer
(This is a SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme)
This course is for you if you are seeking to kickstart your career in AI. It provides a mix of theory and hands-on experience, covering data collection, preprocessing, model development, evaluation, and deployment.

 Programming for AI
(Part of Higher NITEC in Technology – AI Applications by ITE)
Learn to develop, configure, and publish mobile applications using software development methods and tools.

 Applied Artificial Intelligence
(Part of Professional Certificate in AI and Machine Learning Basics by NUS)
This course teaches you how to use AI techniques, focusing on logic and search, to solve real-world problems, including designing fuzzy rule-based expert systems and developing evolutionary algorithms for optimisation.

 Here are more resources for career development or conversion:
1. AI Application Developer Overview: This is a career conversion programme for tech professionals.
2. AI Apprenticeship Programme: This is a 2-month intensive deep-skilling training programme, followed by a 7-month real-world AI Project.
3. AI Singapore Professional Development Courses: These are a set of professional development courses for working adults with basic to intermediate knowledge of AI.

 Back to Profile Selection

 Keep Learning!
According to Salesforce’s Digital Skills Survey in 2023, 60% of global workers are excited about using generative AI in their jobs. In fact, more workers are excited about using it in the workplace (58%) than worried about it replacing their jobs (42%).
As we move forward, let's keep an open mind and stay flexible, embracing the opportunities AI brings to enhance our roles and skills

 As Richard Baldwin, an economist and professor at the Geneva Graduate Institute in Switzerland, put it during the World Economic Forum 2023, “AI won’t take your job. It is somebody using AI who will take your job.”

 Check Out These Resources

Generative Artificial Intelligence for the Tech Workforce: A Resource Guide for Business Owners
Charting a new HR Course: Success Through Digital Transformation
How AI Singapore Taps on Generative AI to Enhance Recruitment
Who Says I’m Too Old For Tech Jobs?

pages.title: How SkillsFuture Singapore Ensures the Quality of Registered Training Providers  | Myskillsfuture.gov.sg
pages.text:
Education, Career and Personal Development

	How SkillsFuture Singapore Ensures the Quality of Registered Training Providers
	Published by: SkillsFuture Singapore

 When you sign up for an SSG-funded course, you’re putting in time and energy into the course. That’s why we ask for your feedback via the Training Quality and Outcomes Measurement (TRAQOM) survey at the end of the course, for you to rate its quality.
In addition, SSG also imposes the Training Provider Quality Assessment (TPQA) on our Registered Training Providers (RTP) to ensure they meet the rigorous quality standards.

 What Is TPQA?
TPQA is a quality assessment to ensure that SSG-funded RTPs
1. adopt a systematic approach to implement good processes and training methods for industry relevant courses,
2. are supported by good administration and governance, and
3. can contribute positive outcomes such as learners’ satisfaction (which are indicated by TRAQOM ratings).
The TPQA requirements cover:
1. Course Administration and Corporate Governance, to ensure that the training provider has the processes to provide administrative support to learners,
2. Course Quality Assurance, to ensure that the training provider has good systems and standards to deliver the learning outcomes, and
3. Outcomes, which measure whether training providers are effectively meeting organisation and course quality outcomes.
The training providers’ adherence to TPQA standards are verified through onsite assessments.

Why is TPQA Relevant to You?
As encapsulated in SSG’s Quality Statements for Individuals, you can play a role and be an effective agent in making your learning meaningful and effective. This includes making informed decisions to choose courses that develop skills for both your career health and workplace’s needs, from reliable training providers.
When TPs undergo TPQA, they must pass the assessment to be able to continue offering courses with SkillsFuture funding. So, when you see an SSG-funded course on MySkillsFuture with a TPQA Grade 1 or 2*, you can enrol with confidence knowing the provider has met stringent standards for quality and credibility.
*Note: TPQA outcome will only be published after Q1 2026. Grade 1 is a higher grade than Grade 2.
Click here to learn more about TPQA.

 Do you have feedback for us on the quality of training providers? Please click here.

pages.title: Cybersecurity Job-Skills Insights into the Singapore Landscape | Myskillsfuture.gov.sg
pages.text:
Jobs-Skills Insights

	Cybersecurity Job-Skills Insights into the Singapore Landscape
	Published by: SkillsFuture Singapore

Singapore's cybersecurity sector has witnessed substantial growth, with its market size skyrocketing to S$2 billion in 2022, more than tripling from an estimated S$570 million in 2015. This comes as no surprise as the number of tech professionals in Singapore rose from 155,500 in 2017 to 201,000 in 2022.
The expansion in the cybersecurity industry is driven by the growing demand for expertise in this field. As technological advancements continue to drive digitalisation across all sectors, cyber threats become greater. Cybercriminals are employing more sophisticated techniques, thereby increasing the frequency of cyberattacks and heightening vulnerability in digital environments. Consequently, this has resulted in a surge for and shortage of cybersecurity workforce and talent globally. Data from LinkedIn Talent Insights for Singapore showed there were 400 – 800 job postings a month from November 2022 to June 2023 for occupations related to “Cyber, “Forensics” and “Security”.

The Cybersecurity Talent Gap
According to the Cybersecurity Workforce Study 2023, skills gaps in cybersecurity are most prevalent in rapidly growing areas such as Cloud Computing Security, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning and Zero Trust Implementation.

A separate report by CyberEdge highlighted the types of cybersecurity skills shortages by job roles, with 40% of organisations experiencing a shortage in IT Security Administrator, followed by IT Security Architect/Engineer roles.

 Efforts to attract, train and upskill individuals to expand and develop the pool of cybersecurity talents are led by the Cyber Security Agency (CSA). In 2021, CSA developed the Singapore Cybersecurity Talent, Innovation & Growth (Cyber TIG) Plan as part of the Cybersecurity Strategy to focus on “Growing a Robust Talent Pipeline” (one of the two foundational enablers) to bridge cybersecurity skills gaps. The plan laid out a spectrum of targeted programmes which included:
(a) Reskilling
The SG Cyber Associates programme offers foundational cybersecurity training tailored for professionals without any prior cybersecurity experience, such as engineers, auditors, lawyers as well as IT and software professionals. It equips individuals with essential cybersecurity skills directly applicable to their respective roles or work environments.

CSA, together with its partners, is offering 10,000 training and exam spaces over three years for participants to obtain entry-level certification in cybersecurity. CSA also plans to offer targeted and customised training for members to meet their specific needs. As a start for this targeted training, CSA will partner with the Institution of Engineers Singapore (IES) to develop and organise courses on specific technology domains such as Internet of Things security for its IES members.
(b) Nurturing the Youth Talent
The SG Cyber Youth programme focuses on guiding youths in their cybersecurity journey, providing students from secondary to tertiary levels exposure and opportunities for students to venture into cybersecurity as a career. Different initiatives include the Youth Cyber Exploration Programme boot camp, where CSA worked with 5 local polytechnics to host more than 400 students across 30 secondary schools.
(c) Skills-based Hiring
A shift in the hiring approach from looking at academic qualifications or past experiences to skills-based hiring can open up more opportunities for both employers and individuals such as fresh graduates. To this end, the Institute for Human Resource Professionals (IHRP) and the TechSkills Accelerator for ITE and Polytechnics Alliance have developed a virtual Skills-Based Hiring Handbook to provide practical guidance and best practices for firms to attract, assess and develop tech talent based on role-specific competencies.
An exemplary initiative is the Skills Pathway for Cybersecurity, which is employer-centric and industry-led, developed with the active participation of 13 founding employers. Guided by an advisory committee, the pathway articulated the cybersecurity skill requirements for individuals aspiring to enter the cybersecurity field, including for mid-career individuals. The pathway also aims to grow the pool of skilled professionals by listing industry-relevant training programmes and recommended industry-recognised certifications to help individuals choose their preferred certifications to enhance their employability and career advancement.

These include SANS Institute’s Hacker Tools, Techniques and Incident Handling course for the Cyber Incident Response and Forensics Analyst role and their Cyber Threat Intelligence course for the Cyber Threat Intelligence Analyst role and the OffSec Penetration Testing with Kali Linux course for the Cyber Penetration Tester role.
To find out more about other industry-relevant certification and courses to enhance your skills development journey, visit https://skillspathway.scs.org.sg.

(d) Specialisations
As technology advances, the need for diverse specialisations within cybersecurity grows. An example would be Operational Technology (OT). Previously, OT systems were traditionally isolated from external networks, which made them relatively secure due to their physical isolation. But with the convergence of OT and Information Technology (IT) systems, OT systems are now often connected to networks and the internet which, in turn, introduce new vulnerabilities while presenting themselves as a potential attack vector.

Today, there’s a heightened focus on implementing robust cybersecurity practices specifically tailored for OT environments to protect against cyber threats and to maintain the integrity, safety and continuity of its industrial processes. This specialisation is reflected in the Operational Technology Cybersecurity Competency Framework (OTCCF) developed by CSA to attract and develop talent for the emerging OT cybersecurity sector in Singapore, especially for specialised cybersecurity roles.

Despite Singapore’s advancements in technology and cybersecurity measures, the country remains susceptible to cyber-attacks. According to the Singapore Cyber Landscape published by CSA, it is reported that ransomware and phishing remains persistent threats locally, with a reported number of 132 and 8,500 cases, respectively, in 2023, excluding unreported cases. Ransomware remains a major issue both in Singapore and globally, with cybersecurity vendors reporting a 13% increase in these incidents worldwide in 2022.
There needs to be a concentrated focus on cybersecurity talent development, not just at the junior level but at the senior leadership level, the reason why the Cybersecurity Leadership Programme was initiated. Soon, job roles such as Chief Information Security Officer (CISO), Chief Risk Officer (CRO) or Chief Technology Officer (CTO) will not be uncommon. Only then can the workforce be ready to meet the evolving challenges head-on in the face of relentless cyber threats impacting our workforce and our lives.
Article contents attributed to David Siah, CSC-SCS, Co-opted Exco Member

 SINGTEL x SIM AI.DEA

 Jump to section

 Overview
 Additional Details
 Pricing

 How to register?

 Submit an enquiry

 Download Programmes Directory

 Course Overview

 Built for senior leaders and decision-makers, this program establishes the strategic clarity required to lead with AI. Participants cut through the hype to identify where AI delivers real business value, how it should align to organisational priorities, and what readiness looks like across people, data, technology, and governance. The course strengthens executive judgement, critical evaluation, and risk-aware decision-making, while grounding leaders in cyber, ethical, and regulatory considerations. By the end of Phase 1, leaders will be positioned to set clear AI ambition, challenge assumptions, and confidently direct secure, high-impact adoption.

 Course benefits

 AI.dea is the only programme that enterprises will need before committing to full-scale AI investment and deployment. The programme focuses on practical, hands-on experimentation to help you identify what truly works for your business. We guide you to design, build and validate a real-world AI proof of concept (PoC) – reducing guesswork, risk and unnecessary cost. By the end of the programme, you will walk away with a clear, actionable AI adoption strategy to plan: business aligned use cases, governance and risk frameworks, as well as execution playbooks that prepare your organisation for scalable, sustainable AI implementation. What sets AI.dea apart for Enterprises1. Real AI Proof-of Concept, Not Theory Apply your learning directly to your own business scenario through a hands-on PoC. Test it safely in a sandbox environment before committing any major investment. 2. Curated Access to Trusted AI Vendors Gain access to a pre-vetted pool of reputable AI solution providers. We help you match with the right vendor to co-develop a PoC tailored to your specific needs. 3. Guided Strategy to Plan JourneyLeaders are guided through a structured approach to define AI ambition, assess readiness, and chart a clear, risk-aware roadmap for confident organisational adoption.4. Buit-In AI Governance, Cybersecurity & Legal ReadinessDevelop a strong foundation in AI governance, risk management, cybersecurity and legal considerations, ensuring your organisation adopts AI safely and responsibly. 5. Government-Supported & Cost Efficient Benefit from significantly subsidized fees through government support, making AI experimentation affordable and accessible for enterprises.

 Course outline

 DAY 1Module 1: Understanding the Future of Business: AI Adoption Landscape Landscape introduction to AI and its various applications Benefits of enhancing your business with AI Target Audience demographics and behavior Case studies of successful AI adoption across different industries (e.g. Retail, E-commerce, government organizations, logistics) Module 2: Setting AI Strategic AmbitionDefine AI’s role for the organisation: augmentation vs. automation Prioritizing business functions (CX, Ops, Finance, etc.) AI maturity levels and strategic ambition mapping Module 3: Reviewing your Organization’s AI Readiness & CapabilityGaining a Perspective: Data Maturity, Infrastructure, Talent Gaps Cross-functional AI Readiness Assessment Cost-benefit Estimation and Productivity Levers Module 4: Tech Architecture and LandscapeTech Architecture and Landscape Overview AI platforms (cloud/on-prem/hybrid), APIs, data flows Core vs. edge AI deployment Interoperability with legacy systems Module 5: Business Use Case Design and EvaluationAligning identified business use cases to strategic objectives ROI projections & success metrics Building PoCs and pilot DAY 2Module 6: AI Cybersecurity & Risk Landscape Threats: model poisoning, prompt injection, data theft Overview of ‘Securing’: Securing ML pipelines, APIs, and endpoints Data residency, privacy, and regulatory risks Module 7: AI Governance & Ethical OversightBias mitigation, transparency, explainability AI audit readiness and compliance (e.g., PDPA, GDPR) Governance committee & escalation paths Module 8: Execution Plan & KPI Alignment Road mapping: pilots to enterprise-wide rollout KPI setting (efficiency, productivity, service) Culture, change management & communications Course ScheduleRun #Program Commencement DateProgram End Date115-Jan-2616-Jan-26226-Jan-2627-Jan-26305-Feb-2606-Feb-26424-Feb-2625-Feb-26516-Mar-2617-Mar-26

 Duration
 2 days

 Who should attend?

 Level 5 - Senior Managers & DirectorsLevel 6 - C-Suite

 Programme leader
 Michelle Yeo is a seasoned strategist to some of the world's leading businesses and brands senior management teams. Her intimate working knowledge of business across Deep Tech, FMCG, Retail, Business, Digital Transformation and Brand Management enable her to quickly pinpoint the applications of AI for different industries and functions.Michelle is adept with reading and translating data into business insights and strategies and has worked with and consulted for world-class companies such as The Coca-Cola Company, LEGO, Procter & Gamble, Unilever, Mead Johnson, Cerebos, Singtel, Fraser & Neave, NTT, just to name a few.Having been a Co-Founder a few tech start- ups dealing in AI and data analytics, Michelle has gained a few accolades – including winning the first prize in predictive analytics for fault detection (87% accuracy) in the ASTAR (ARTC) competition, and being a guest speaker in conferences in Shanghai, Berlin, Singapore, etc. She is also a Senior Consultant with a few organizations where she spearheads business and strategic initiatives including driving the workings and understandings within AI, Data Analytics as well as cyber fraud and scams.

 Jump to section

 Overview

 Pricing

 How to register?

 Submit an enquiry

 Download Programmes Directory

 Course Overview

 This alarming trend is because the most damaging cyberattacks don't just breach systems – they increasingly exploit human vulnerabilities through deceitful tactics like business scams, especially sophisticated business email compromise (BEC) schemes. That’s why cultivating a resilient cybersecurity culture fundamentally starts with your employees, your first line of defense.Our Cyber Resilience Programme, developed in collaboration with Singtel Cybersecurity Institute—part of the SkillsFuture Queen Bee network, helps organisations equip their people with vital cybersecurity awareness and skills.

 Course benefits

 With a unique emphasis on sensemaking skills and attacker empathy, the programme equips your employees with:1. The skills and sensemaking ability to spot and thwart scams2. The ability to think like attackers and act like defenders3. The confidence to identify, report, and intervene in real-time

 Course outline

 Day 1FROM AWARENESS TO ACTION: RESPONSIBILITY IN SCAM INTERVENTIONIn this module, you will learn about assuming responsibility in combating scams by embracing a mindset shift. The module covers:• Understand how you can contribute to scam identification, stalling, or prevention by being aware and understanding the intentions of all parties involved• Recognise and analyse scammer manipulation tactics to enhance your ability to prevent and intervene effectivelyNAVIGATING DIGITAL THREATS WITH SENSEMAKING AND SYSTEMS THINKINGIn this module, you will be introduced to sensemaking to help one spot potential scams while also applying systems thinking to grasp the intricacies of the information gathered.The module covers:• An introduction to the concepts of Sense Making• Understanding an attacker's lifecycle• Application of Systems Thinking TheoriesDay 2DECISION MAKING AND FORMULATIONIn this module, you will learn about effective decision making and avoiding blind spots.The module covers:• Utilising primary and secondary data for making of data-driven decisions• Identifying blind spots to mitigate adverse effects on decision making efficacyHANDS-ON EXERCISESAll learners will get to experience the different types of cyber scams and learn how modern cyber scams work. The activities cover:• Live demonstration of the various cyber scams• Defensive anti-scam techniques• Table-top exercise with role play on scam situation

 Duration
 2 day

 Who should attend?

 Level 3 - New ManagersLevel 4 - ManagersLevel 5 - Senior Managers & Directors

Professional Development

 Catalyse Your Career

 Catalyse your career growth with SIM Academy’s library of industry-aligned courses in leadership, innovation, sustainability, soft skills, and more, designed to equip you for the future of work.

Learn for Life Thrive for Life.

 SIM Academy Programme Directory 2026

 SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme

 Critical Core Skills programmes

 Catalyse Your Career Growth with SIM Academy

 In a world where AI can replicate tasks and automation can replace processes, the true differentiator isn’t efficiency and technical know-how. It’s human capability: the ability to sensemake, adapt, and create value where machines cannot.

That’s why lifelong learning isn’t an afterthought — it’s the engine for growth. At SIM Academy, our programmes go beyond upskilling. They catalyse transformation, preparing you for the next turning point in your career and equipping you to take the next step with confidence.

 Why SIM Academy?

 As your transformative learning partner, we are here to help you learn for life and thrive for life. Our mission is to catalyse your growth by equipping you with the skillsets and mindsets that will keep you future-ready.

At SIM Academy, our programmes are designed to catalyse personal and professional growth through a distinctive approach that connects industry-relevant knowledge to practice.

With SIM Academy, lifelong learning is not an option — it is the engine of growth for your career.

 Industry-Aligned Training Programmes

 Our programmes are specially designed and curated in collaboration with industry partners to ensure we impart skills that are truly needed by the industry.

 Contextualised Learning Solutions

 Programmes are meticulously contextualised, taking into account the nuances of your industry, business strategies and organisational culture.

Explore a wide range of courses offered by SIM Academy here

 Application-Focused Coaching*

 Registration & Payment Process

 What you need to know

 Refer to the step-by-step guide below on registering and making payment for professional development courses at SIM.

 1
 Explore & Register

 2
 Class Confirmation

 3
 Make Payment

 Step 1 of 3

 Explore & Register

	Explore the full list of available courses on our course listing page.
	View professional courses
	Once you have shortlisted a course, click on the ‘Register Now’ button on the course details page. You will be redirected to the SIM Online.

	Create an account if you are new to the portal, or log in with your email address and password.
	Upon successful registration, you will receive a registration acknowledgement email.

 Step 2 of 3

 Class Confirmation

	You will receive a class confirmation email approximately one week before class commences.
	SIM Academy reserves the right to cancel or reschedule a course due to unforeseen circumstances.
	For withdrawal or deferment, email the Programme Admin in-charge at least 14 days prior to the course start date.
	Refund policy for withdrawal:

 Written notification received 14 days or more before commencement: 100% refund
 Written notification received less than 14 days before commencement: 75% refund
 After commencement / No show: No refund

 Step 3 of 3

 Make Payment

 When your class is confirmed, SIM Academy will send you an official e-invoice. The payee will receive an official e-receipt at no charge upon successful course fees payment.

Electronic invoice format

View a sample of the invoice (PDF 168KB)

Payment methods

We offer the following payment methods:

	PayNow (Platform fee waived till further notice)
	Bank transfer
	Flywire (Non-refundable platform fee applies)

 Overseas transfer
 Credit Cards

 The Flywire platform fee is a strictly non-refundable fee. This includes, but is not limited to, situations where the applicant withdraws, is withdrawn, or is not accepted into the programme, as well as circumstances in which the programme is postponed or cancelled by the organiser or any third party. The platform fee is charged by Flywire for payment processing and is not retained by the programme provider; therefore, it cannot be returned under any circumstance

pages.title: Training and Adult Education Industry Transformation Map
pages.text: HomeTraining & Adult EducationTraining and Adult Education Industry Transformation MapTraining and Adult Education Industry Transformation MapDeveloping an agile Training and Adult Education sector that powers workforce transformationLast updated 4 June 2025What is the Training and Adult Education Industry Transformation Map? The Training and Adult Education (TAE) sector plays a major role in raising the employability of Singaporeans and supporting businesses in capability building. The Training and Adult Education Industry Transformation Map (TAE ITM) 2025 aims to develop an innovative high-quality, market-responsive TAE sector that supports transformation across all sectors.Building on the good foundations laid out in the TAE ITM 2020, this plan aims to transform the TAE sector to meet Singapore’s current and future training needs, while creating new opportunities to grow the sector. 2025 Key Strategies1. Improve industry-relevance and market responsiveness of trainingFor the TAE sector to effectively support transformation of other sectors, training has to align with industry needs and demand. SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) will continue to work closely with the industry through existing partnerships such as the SkillsFuture Queen Bees initiative. We will develop and support new collaborate models between training providers and enterprises.SSG will also continue to drive skills recognition and skills credentialling through greater involvement by companies, unions, associations and professional bodies. For example, SSG launched the Skills Development Partners initiative in 2022 to work with Trade Associations and Chambers in identifying needed sectoral skills more responsively and better link skills development with ongoing enterprise transformation and job redesign within the sector. The initiative also aims to help enterprises better recognise employees’ skills acquisition and skills mastery.2. Innovate and digitalise at scaleTo provide training that support business performance and meet the skills needs of the industries they serve, the TAE sector will have to scale up their innovation and digitalisation efforts.The COVID-19 pandemic has brought about opportunities for digital and technology-based training. Building on this momentum, SSG will support training providers in the development and adoption of innovative learning approaches. For example, in the area of e-learning, by co-funding promising training innovations through iN.LEARN 2.0, which consists of 3 programmes – innovation incubator (innovPlus), innovation accelerator (innovSpur), and an innovation Sandbox to temporarily remove regulatory obstacles that impedes innovation.SSG will promote process digitalisation to help training providers reduce the burden of training administration and improve customer service, as part of the continued effort from the TAE ITM 2020.SSG will work closely with the Institute for Adult Learning (IAL) as the National Centre of Excellence for Adult Learning (NCAL) to intensify the translation of andragogy research into improved and effective teaching practices and tools that training providers can easily implement.3. Invest in adult educators and sectoral capabilitiesIn view of the emerging skills required by the TAE sector, training providers will need to invest in building a strong and skilled TAE workforce.SSG worked with IAL to revamp the Workforce Skills Qualifications Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance (WSQ ACLP). This enables aspiring adult educators to acquire the essentials of classroom facilitation, tech-enabled learning and workplace learning delivery and assessment in a shorter amount of time.Aspiring adult educators will be able to complete the revamped WSQ ACLP in three months, compared to the six to nine months required previously. SSG will also collaborate with IAL and industry stakeholders to provide more support for our adult educators to develop their skillsets, particularly in areas that are growing in importance and demand. These include skills in the areas of EdTech, workplace learning, and career coaching.4. Internationalise to strengthen sector resilienceInternationalisation will enable the TAE sector to diversify its provisions and test its capabilities in overseas market. SSG, in partnership with Enterprise Singapore and Ministry of Trade and Industry, will work with promising training providers to export Continuing Education and Training to the region and beyond.Back to top

pages.title: Training Quality And Outcomes Measurement (TRAQOM)
pages.text: HomeTraining & Adult EducationTraining Quality And Outcomes Measurement (TRAQOM)Training Quality And Outcomes Measurement (TRAQOM)Empowering learners and uplifting training standards through feedback and impact measurementLast updated 4 June 2025What is Training Quality and Outcomes Measurement (TRAQOM)?TRAQOM developed by SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) aims to enhance the quality of SSG-funded Continuing Education and Training (CET) programmes. TRAQOM captures feedback from learners through two surveys. These surveys assess course quality and how well the training supports career growth for courses funded by SSG and those eligible for SkillsFuture Credit. Why TRAQOM matters?TRAQOM can help:Learners make more informed training choices by referencing the TRAQOM ratings for courses listed on the MySkillsFuture portal;Training Providers improve course offerings by referencing their TRAQOM survey data; andGovernment agencies like MOE and SSG to review and inform policies and regulations (e.g., SSG’s funding policies).How is the TRAQOM administered? The two TRAQOM surveys are administered by Qualtrics, an SSG-appointed vendor:Course Quality Survey: Measures learners’ perception of the training quality and should be administered by the Training Provider via QR code, usually on the last day of the course or via email or SMS, a day after the last day of the course.Jobs/Careers Impact Survey: Measures learners’ perception of how the course has impacted their work and careers. This survey is administered three months after the course ends.View the questions here.Scan the course quality survey QR codeIf you are a learner, scan this QR code to complete the Quality Survey.As part of the login verification, you would be required to key in:Last four NRIC/FIN characters; andCourse Run ID (either six- or seven-digit)Access the Course Quality Survey here.Learners have up to four tries to key in the required login verification information and complete the survey.Receiving survey invites via email and SMSIf the Quality Surveys is not attempted or completed via QR Code on the last day of course, learners will receive an email or SMS (if email is not available) invitation with the link to access the survey.The Jobs/Careers Impact Survey is administered solely via email or SMS (if email is not available).Sample screenshots of the invitesJobs/Careers Impact Survey is administered solely via email or SMS (if email is not available). Refer to sample screenshots of the invites here.Email phishing and SMS scams alertsThe TRAQOM Survey invite links are shortened with a "go.gov.sg” domain (i.e., https://go.gov.sg/ssgsurvey). This is to assure learners that the survey links are legitimate and safe. Do note that TRAQOM Surveys are administered via Qualtrics. Hence, upon clicking the .gov.sg link, learners will be redirected to Qualtrics (i.e., a site with a “.qualtrics.com” domain).Please note that, as part of the TRAQOM Survey administration, SSG will never ask for: (i) personal identification (e.g., NRIC), and (ii) financial and payment information (e.g., bank account information, credit card / debit card details and SMS OTPs).If you were wrongly administered any of the TRAQOM Surveys, you may alert us via the SSG feedback portal: https://service-portal.skillsfuture.gov.sg/s/feedback/.If you suspect that you are a victim of fraud or receive emails and SMSes from unknown sources, you may report it via the ScamShield mobile app, an initiative by the Singapore Police Force and the National Crime Prevention Council. The features of ScamShield include the following:Block scam calls – ScamShield compares an incoming call against a list maintained by the Singapore Police Force to determine if the number has been used for illegal purposes and blocks it.Detect scam SMSes – When you receive an SMS from an unknown contact, ScamShield will determine if the SMS is a scam using an on-device algorithm, and filter the messages to a junk SMS folder. Scam SMSes will be sent to NCPC and SPF for collation to keep app updated and help protect others from such scam calls and messages.Report scam messages – You can also report scam messages from other chat apps such as WhatsApp, Wechat, IMO, Viber, etc. You can forward the messages via ScamShield's in-app reporting function.For more information on ScamShield, please visit: https://www.scamshield.org.sgFor technical assistance and/or clarifications, please contact our appointed vendor’s (Rolling Arrays’) helpdesk at 6980 6493 or email qualtricssupport@rollingarrays.com.sg. You may refer to our Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) as well.If you have any other enquiries, please visit our feedback portal.Back to top
